Guru Gobind Singh kept a baaj (white hawk/falcon) to symbolize the high moral character, sovereignty, and fearless, free-spirited nature of the Khalsa. Known as "Chittay Baaja Wala" (Keeper of the White Hawk), he used the bird to teach lessons of self-reliance, high-flying ambition with humility, and unwavering spiritual-political resilience against tyranny. 
The Heritage Lab
The Heritage Lab
 +4
Key Reasons for Keeping the Baaj:
Symbol of the Khalsa: The falcon represents the ideal Khalsa: independent, courageous, and never accepting subjugation.
Freedom and Sovereignty: Just as a baaj cannot be easily caged, it signifies that the Khalsa cannot be enslaved or controlled by external forces.
Self-Reliance: The hawk hunts its own food, teaching Sikhs to be self-sufficient, confident, and not dependent on others.
Royal Authority: In an era where only royalty held falcons, the Guru used this to empower his followers, defying Mughal authority and asserting the dignity of the common person.
Spiritual Lessons: It represents high spiritual aspirations (soaring high) combined with humility (keeping its gaze on the ground).

Your Majesty,�Greetings of the day.
My name is Yashvardhan Yadav, a respected resident of the United Arab Emirates since 27/05/2024. With due respect, I wish to bring to your kind attention a matter that has caused me great distress and hardship.
I applied for a driving license in August 2025, and now in 2026, I find myself still unsuccessful due to repeated failures by the driving school. I strongly feel that the institute has been unfairly failing students without valid or genuine reasons. To date, I have paid approximately AED 5,800 in fees. Most recently, I was failed in the final road test despite not making any significant mistake, and the examiner’s behavior was also discourteous and unprofessional.
Your Majesty, until this experience, my journey in the UAE under your gracious leadership has been truly wonderful. However, this incident has left me feeling a sense of injustice. I have exhausted all my savings paying the driving school’s fees and am now in a situation where I do not even have sufficient funds to book a flight to return home.
Obtaining a driving license was my only hope to secure a stable livelihood and continue surviving here. Unfortunately, I have now lost that opportunity as well.
I humbly request your kind consideration and intervention in this matter, so that justice may be served.
With deepest respect and regards,
Yashvardhan Yadav
